description_long: This butcher shop consists of four market-style stalls arranged in a gentle arc, each covered with a red and white striped canopy. The stalls are constructed from wooden frames and feature open fronts for customer interaction, with some containing visible barrels, crates, and baskets underneath or beside the counters. At the center of the arc is a circular stone well or chopping block, adding a rustic touch to the scene. The canopies are vibrant and eye-catching, providing shade and a traditional market atmosphere. The stalls are open on the sides and front, allowing easy access to the goods displayed within. The overall setup suggests a temporary or outdoor market butcher shop rather than a permanent building.
description: A group of four wooden butcher stalls with red and white striped canopies, arranged in an arc around a central stone well or block. The stalls are open-fronted and display various containers and market goods beneath the counters.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four stalls with open fronts, striped canopies, and the central stone well; market goods are visible under the counters.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the stalls and the arrangement of the canopies; the central well is more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the backs of the stalls and the containers beneath the counters; the canopies' stripes are clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the side of the stalls and the open arrangement; the central well and the front of one stall are visible.
